# The Good Ship

> Strategy, technology & resilience for social purpose organisations.

The Good Ship is a consultancy founded by Tom Campbell Watson, based in North East England. We help charities, social enterprises, B Corps, and mission-driven organisations navigate uncertainty through strategy, technology, data, and open tools.

## Services

- AI & Technology Strategy: Helping organisations adopt AI and technology thoughtfully
- Data Strategy, Architecture & Design: Building data foundations that serve mission
- Organisational Resilience: Strengthening organisations to weather uncertainty
- Product & Team Development: Building capable teams and useful products
- Learning Partnerships & Coaching: Ongoing support and capability building
- Open Infrastructure: Building shared tools and platforms for the sector
- Shipyard (good-ship.co.uk/shipyard/): Bespoke tool-building for organisations — discovery, design, development, and deployment of tools you own, built around how you actually work. Built with open-source software and EU alternatives to improve tech and data sovereignty and resilience. Two modes: "Ship It" (we build, you get tools) or "Learn & Ship" (your team builds, we guide)

## Tools

- TechFreedom (techfreedom.eu): Tech sovereignty programme for social purpose organisations
- Glade (ourglade.app): Governance platform that treats every decision as a seed — traceable, reviewable institutional memory
- Impact Path (impact-path.vercel.app): Build your impact strategy through guided conversation
- Swells (swells.app): Platform for observations, signals and reflections — sense what's emerging before it becomes obvious
- Drift (driftforms.app): Intelligent forms that adapt to every respondent, as natural as conversation
- Bearing (findbearing.org): AI model discovery and comparison tool
- Tending (tending.network): Relationship and network management for social purpose organisations — log connections in plain language, map them as a living network
- Open Org (good-ship.co.uk/open-org): Data standard for self-sovereign organisational profiles, living strategies and published ideas
- Flowlance (flowlance.io): Finance and project management for freelancers
- llmstxt.social: AI-readable documentation platform
- Open Recommendations (openrecs.com): Analysis tool for social sector reports
- Stackmap (stackmap.org): Open-source tool to document tech infrastructure — systems, costs, risks, responsibilities
- The List (the-list.uk): UK funding database
- Map My Patch (mapmypatch.co.uk): Neighbourhood mapping tool
- Questions for Action (questionsforaction.com): Prioritisation tool

## Frameworks & Resources

- Garden of Ideas: Collaborative ideation framework
- Theory of Change — Demystified: Simplified theory of change approach
- Principles of Good Data: Data ethics and quality principles
- Organisational Resilience Toolkit: Practical resilience tools
- Collaboration Spectrum: Framework for partnership approaches
- Simple Facilitation Tools for Teams: Meeting and workshop facilitation
- Responsible AI for Food Systems (good-ship.co.uk/responsible-ai/): A provocation and curated resources for organisations working in food systems building an AI policy in the open — created for the Food Ethics Council
